CertifiedData Payments (CDP) — machine-facing repository guide

OVERVIEW
CertifiedData Payments (CDP) is the provenance-aware commerce and settlement contract for the CertifiedData ecosystem.
This repository contains the PUBLIC developer and machine-facing contract layer for CDP.
This repository does NOT contain private payment execution internals.

PUBLIC / PRIVATE BOUNDARY
Public: API contract, event contract, schemas, SDKs, examples, test vectors, docs, mock server, discovery manifests
Private: payment processor secret material, routing heuristics, treasury internals, compliance internals, DB migrations, internal payout execution logic, signing key custody internals

HOW TO READ THIS REPOSITORY
1. api-manifest.json
2. schemas/capabilities/capabilities.json
3. constraints.json
4. support-matrix.json
5. openapi/certifieddata-payments-v1.openapi.yaml
6. asyncapi/certifieddata-payments-events-v1.asyncapi.yaml
7. schemas/common/* and schemas/resources/*
8. schemas/state-machines/*
9. examples/json and test-vectors
10. docs markdown (explanation/navigation only)

SOURCE OF TRUTH
REST: openapi/certifieddata-payments-v1.openapi.yaml
Events: asyncapi/certifieddata-payments-events-v1.asyncapi.yaml
Shared models: schemas/common/* and schemas/resources/*
Lifecycle: schemas/state-machines/*
Errors: schemas/errors/error-catalog.json
Capabilities: schemas/capabilities/capabilities.json
Constraints: constraints.json
Compatibility: support-matrix.json

DISCOVERY FILES
Primary: api-manifest.json
Secondary: docs/docs-index.json, schemas/enums/event-types.json, schemas/enums/payment-rails.json, schemas/enums/transaction-statuses.json

RESOURCE ID PREFIXES
py_ payee | pya_ payee alias | pydst_ payout destination | cus_ customer
inv_ invoice | pi_ payment intent | tx_ transaction | stl_ settlement
rf_ refund | evt_ event | wh_ webhook endpoint | idem_ idempotency record

CORE DIFFERENTIATOR
CDP is provenance-aware. Transactions, settlements, events, and related objects may be linked to:
artifact_id, certificate_id, decision_id, dataset_id, model_id, output_id, receipt_hash, external_reference, provenance_metadata
Provenance link shape: schemas/resources/provenance-link-set.schema.json

MACHINE-READABLE OPERATING RULE
When a concept appears in both prose and a machine-readable artifact, prefer the machine-readable artifact.

REST SURFACE
Canonical: openapi/certifieddata-payments-v1.openapi.yaml
Core families: payees, payout destinations, customers, invoices, payment intents, transactions, settlements, refunds, webhook endpoints, events, capabilities, health

EVENT SURFACE
Canonical: asyncapi/certifieddata-payments-events-v1.asyncapi.yaml
Envelope fields: id, event_type, event_version, api_version, occurred_at, environment, livemode, resource_type, resource_id, data, provenance, request_context

RESOURCE INDEX
payee         | py_     | schemas/resources/payee.schema.json          | POST/GET /v1/payees
payee_alias   | pya_    | schemas/resources/payee-alias.schema.json     | POST/GET /v1/payees/{id}/aliases
payout_dest   | pydst_  | schemas/resources/payout-destination.schema.json | POST/GET /v1/payees/{id}/payout-destinations
customer      | cus_    | schemas/resources/customer.schema.json        | POST/GET /v1/customers
invoice       | inv_    | schemas/resources/invoice.schema.json         | POST/GET/PATCH /v1/invoices
payment_intent| pi_     | schemas/resources/payment-intent.schema.json  | POST/GET /v1/payment-intents
transaction   | tx_     | schemas/resources/transaction.schema.json     | POST/GET /v1/transactions
settlement    | stl_    | schemas/resources/settlement.schema.json      | POST/GET /v1/settlements
refund        | rf_     | schemas/resources/refund.schema.json          | POST/GET /v1/refunds
event         | evt_    | schemas/resources/event.schema.json           | GET /v1/events
webhook_ep    | wh_     | schemas/resources/webhook-endpoint.schema.json| POST/GET/PATCH /v1/webhook-endpoints

EVENT INDEX
payee.created              | payee         | POST /v1/payees
payee.updated              | payee         | PATCH /v1/payees/{id}
payee.alias.created        | payee_alias   | POST /v1/payees/{id}/aliases
payout_destination.created | payout_dest   | POST /v1/payees/{id}/payout-destinations
invoice.created            | invoice       | POST /v1/invoices
invoice.finalized          | invoice       | POST /v1/invoices/{id}/finalize
invoice.voided             | invoice       | POST /v1/invoices/{id}/void
payment_intent.created     | payment_intent| POST /v1/payment-intents
payment_intent.confirmed   | payment_intent| POST /v1/payment-intents/{id}/confirm
payment_intent.canceled    | payment_intent| POST /v1/payment-intents/{id}/cancel
transaction.created        | transaction   | POST /v1/transactions
transaction.captured       | transaction   | POST /v1/transactions/{id}/capture
transaction.canceled       | transaction   | POST /v1/transactions/{id}/cancel
transaction.links_attached | transaction   | POST /v1/transactions/{id}/attach-links
settlement.created         | settlement    | POST /v1/settlements
settlement.submitted       | settlement    | POST /v1/settlements/{id}/submit
settlement.succeeded       | settlement    | async — platform event
settlement.failed          | settlement    | async — platform event
refund.created             | refund        | POST /v1/refunds
refund.succeeded           | refund        | async — platform event
refund.failed              | refund        | async — platform event

WORKFLOW INDEX
payee-onboarding:          POST /v1/payees → POST /v1/payees/{id}/aliases → POST /v1/payees/{id}/payout-destinations
certified-artifact-purchase: POST /v1/payment-intents → POST /v1/transactions → POST /v1/transactions/{id}/attach-links → POST /v1/transactions/{id}/capture
settlement:                POST /v1/settlements → POST /v1/settlements/{id}/submit
refund:                    POST /v1/refunds

TEST VECTOR INDEX
test-vectors/webhook-signature.valid.json     — valid HMAC-SHA256 webhook signature
test-vectors/webhook-signature.invalid.json   — invalid signature (tampered v1 value)
test-vectors/idempotency.same-request-replay.json — idempotent replay, same key + payload
test-vectors/idempotency.payload-mismatch.json    — same key, different payload → 409
test-vectors/provenance.attach-before-capture.valid.json   — attach links before capture → 200
test-vectors/provenance.attach-after-capture.invalid.json  — attach links after capture → 409
test-vectors/event.transaction.captured.fixture.json       — canonical event fixture

VERSIONING RULES
API version: 2025-01-01 (passed as CDP-API-Version header)
Schema versions: semver in each schema file
SDK versions: see versions.json
Never rename stable enum values — add new ones with deprecation notice only
Never remove fields — mark deprecated with a minor version bump

ENVIRONMENTS
sandbox | live — environment-scoped, non-portable
Sandbox: cdp_test_... API keys, livemode: false
Live: cdp_live_... API keys, livemode: true

SDKS
TypeScript: @certifieddata/payments — packages/typescript-sdk
Python: certifieddata-payments — packages/python-sdk

ENUM REGISTRIES
schemas/enums/event-types.json | schemas/enums/payment-rails.json | schemas/enums/transaction-statuses.json

LIMITS AND CONSTRAINTS
Canonical: constraints.json
Key limits: idempotency key max 128 chars, metadata max 20 keys, provenance links immutable after capture,
webhook signature tolerance 300 seconds, pagination default 20 max 100

AGENT GUIDANCE
Prefer explicit enums, structured examples, source-of-truth artifacts, constraints manifests, state machine JSON.
Do not infer unsupported payment rails, unsupported statuses, private routing logic, compliance internals, internal service topology.
If ambiguity: read manifest → capability manifest → OpenAPI/AsyncAPI → schema → state machine → prose docs
